New Claude Code Hijacks Mouse Clicks in Your Terminal? Kill It with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS

When you're working in Claude Code and try to select text in the terminal or click a link in an error message, the clicks get hijacked by Claude Code itself. This post walks through the difference between CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE and CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS, how to add it to your .zshrc, and the one-off bypass hotkeys for iTerm2, macOS Terminal, and VS Code when you don't want to touch your config.
Want to Start Vibe Coding? The Fundamentals Beginners Should Get Down First

Lately a lot of friends have been asking me, "I want to use AI to build things too — where do I start?" And what they're really asking about is Vibe Coding. This post is about the fundamentals beginners should get down before diving into Vibe Coding — Git, command line, UI Frameworks, and so on. So you don't get halfway through and find you're stuck not knowing how to communicate with the AI.
How to Clear the Terminal Screen (Mac and Windows)

Whether you're on Mac or Windows, the terminal piles up output fast and it gets noisy. Quick note on the commands and keyboard shortcuts that clear the screen — including the distinction between "actually wiping scrollback" vs. "just scrolling the cursor down so the screen looks empty".
Mac: "zsh: command not found: xxx" — Two Common Causes and Fixes

Hit "zsh: command not found: hexo" or similar on Mac after installing a global npm package? Two flavors of this issue, both common — one if you use nvm (npm prefix conflicting with nvm), one if you don't (zsh PATH not picking up npm's global bin directory). This post covers both fixes step by step.
Windows "running scripts is disabled on this system" — How to Fix PowerShell Execution Policy

Hit "running scripts is disabled on this system, so cannot load..." in Windows PowerShell? Windows defaults the Execution Policy to Restricted (especially on Windows 10 PowerShell), which blocks script execution as a security measure. This post explains the Execution Policy levels (AllSigned, Bypass, Default, RemoteSigned, Restricted, Undefined, Unrestricted) and how to switch to RemoteSigned so npm, vue, gulp, webpack, etc. work normally.
The Complete Mac Terminal Setup: iTerm2 + Zsh + Oh My Zsh + Powerlevel10k

A complete walkthrough of how I customize my Mac terminal — installing Homebrew, iTerm2, Zsh, Oh My Zsh, useful Zsh plugins (zsh-completions, zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting), themes (agnoster, Powerlevel10k), iTerm2 color schemes, and the edge cases that bite you (nvm, Homebrew PATH, VSCode font). Plus my full .zshrc for reference.
Customize Windows Terminal with oh-my-posh (PowerShell Prompt + Windows Terminal Setup)

Staring at the same plain PowerShell prompt all day gets old. This post walks through giving Windows PowerShell a git-style prompt with oh-my-posh, plus installing and customizing Windows Terminal (the newer Microsoft terminal) — themes, fonts, background image. Heads up — this article was written in 2019; oh-my-posh has since moved to v3+ which uses different config commands.
